title: FrameReader.max_size not enforced during buffer growth
description:
**Location:** python/src/lichen/interface/framing.py:78-80

**Risk:** The `FrameReader` stores a `max_size` parameter but the `feed()` method extends the buffer without checking total size. A slow sender could feed bytes one at a time, growing the buffer past max_size before a length field is parsed.

**Trigger:** Slow-drip attack: send bytes slowly, never completing a frame, growing the buffer indefinitely.

**Bad behavior:** Memory exhaustion as buffer grows without bound.

**Note:** The max_size check on line 103 only applies after parsing the length field from a complete header. The buffer can grow arbitrarily large with partial headers.
